description: Native app surfaces gating (docs/design/native-app-surfaces.md): mobile-context gates validated on the general matrix. This spec is deterministic on EVERY host — as of phase A5 (mobile web), a SUPPORTED browser+platform pair proceeds to real toolchain/device work, so the always-SKIP assertions here use UNSUPPORTED pairs, which the mobile-browser support matrix rejects before any SDK/emulator/simulator detection. No host lazily installs a toolchain or boots a device here. Native mobile app runs and supported mobile-web runs (which PASS on capable hosts) are exercised in the apps-android, apps-ios, mobile-web-android, and mobile-web-ios groups, not here.

tests.description: An android context with an unsupported browser (firefox) is SKIPPED by the mobile-browser support matrix, naming chrome as the supported Android browser. Fires before any SDK detection, so the outcome is deterministic on every host and never triggers a toolchain install or emulator boot.

An ios context with an unsupported browser (chrome) is SKIPPED by the mobile-browser support matrix, naming safari as the supported iOS browser. Fires before any simulator detection, so the outcome is deterministic on every host — including macOS — and never boots a simulator. Supported mobile-web runs (safari on ios) are exercised in the mobile-web-ios group.
